en.extract:
The purple-capped fruit dove or Pohnpei fruit dove (Ptilinopus ponapensis), is a species of bird in the family Columbidae found on Chuuk and Pohnpei in the Caroline Islands. It was formerly considered as a subspecies of the crimson-crowned fruit dove. Its natural habitats are subtropical or tropical moist lowland forests and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.


References

Cibois, A., J.-C. Thibault, C. Bonillo, C.E. Filardi, D. Watling, and E. Pasquet. 2014. Phylogeny and biogeography of the fruit doves (Aves: Columbidae). Molecular Phylogenetics and Evolution 70: 442–453.

The '''purple-capped fruit dove''' or '''Pohnpei fruit dove''' ('''''Ptilinopus ponapensis'''''), is a species of [[bird]] in the family [[Columbidae]] found on [[Chuuk Lagoon|Chuuk]] and [[Pohnpei]] in the [[Caroline Islands]]. It was formerly considered as a subspecies of the [[crimson-crowned fruit dove]].<ref>{{Cite journal|title=IOC World Bird List 6.3|url=http://www.worldbirdnames.org/ioc-lists/crossref|journal=IOC World Bird List Datasets|doi=10.14344/ioc.ml.6.3|doi-access=free}}</ref> Its natural [[habitat]]s are subtropical or tropical moist lowland [[forest]]s and subtropical or tropical mangrove forests.
